step_by_step_thinking:
\boxed{A}
**Clinical Reasoning:** Although none of the retrieved documents explicitly define ADP-ribosylation as a post-translational modification (PTM), one entry states that "hypoxanthine is specifically characterized as a PARP inhibitory agent that blocks poly(ADP-ribose) polymerase activity." Since PARP enzymes catalyze ADP-ribosylation—a well-established biochemical process involving the transfer of ADP-ribose units to proteins—the described mechanism implies that ADP-ribosylation occurs enzymatically and modifies protein function, fitting the definition of a PTM. Therefore, despite limited textual detail, biological context confirms its relevance as a regulated molecular modification.
